setting.note: Balzac's Paris of 1819, climbed one address at a time. The shabby Maison Vauquer stands in the poor Latin-Quarter streets behind the Panthéon; from there Rastignac reaches up to Mme de Beauseant's salon in the Faubourg Saint-Germain and the new money of the Chaussee d'Antin, and buries Goriot on the heights of Pere-Lachaise. Balzac maps the whole social ladder onto real streets, and it brings the founder of the realist novel into the atlas.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Honore de Balzac, first published 1835; the author died in 1850.
rights.translation: English translation by Ellen Marriage; the translation is in the public domain in the US and UK/EU.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the US and UK/EU.
rights.attribution: Honore de Balzac, Father Goriot (1835), trans. Ellen Marriage. Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The gilded salons of the Faubourg Saint-Germain, the shining world Rastignac wants. A dark gold that clears WCAG AA on paper.
